q.en: Who proved the Germ Theory of Disease, which states that contact with harmful microbes causes disease?
q.zh: 谁证明了疾病细菌学说（即接触有害微生物会导致疾病）？

explain.en: The book states: 'Modern medicine owes a lot to Louis Pasteur. He proved the Germ Theory of Disease, which states that contact with harmful microbes is the cause of disease.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：「现代医学很大程度上归功于路易·巴斯德。他证明了疾病细菌学说，即接触有害微生物是致病的原因。」

q.en: What heating process, named after Pasteur, is used on fresh milk and canned food to kill organisms?
q.zh: 以巴斯德命名、用于牛奶和罐头食品杀菌的加热处理过程叫什么？

options.en: sterilization according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 灭菌（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says Pasteur discovered that heating sealed food to a specific temperature kills organisms, and 'This heating process is called pasteurization. Today it is used on fresh milk and canned food.'
explain.zh: 书中说巴斯德发现将密封食品加热到特定温度可以杀死微生物，「这一加热过程称为巴氏杀菌法。如今它被用于鲜奶和罐头食品。」

q.en: Which of the four groups of microbes causes common infections like ringworm and athlete's foot?
q.zh: 四大类微生物中，哪一类会引起癣和脚气等常见感染？

explain.en: Under 'Fungi' the book says: 'the microscopic type of harmful fungi that invade our body cause irritating, common infections like ringworm and athlete's foot.'
explain.zh: 在「真菌」一节，书中写道：「侵入人体的微小有害真菌会引起恼人的常见感染，如癣和脚气。」

q.en: According to the book, which microbe is responsible for many cases of food poisoning?
q.zh: 根据本书，许多食物中毒病例是由哪种微生物引起的？

explain.en: The book states: 'Salmonella is the bacteria responsible for many cases of food poisoning.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：「沙门氏菌是导致许多食物中毒病例的细菌。」

q.en: In the glossary, what does the word 'mutate' mean?
q.zh: 在词汇表中，「mutate」（变异）一词是什么意思？

options.en: to hide from the immune system

explain.en: The glossary defines 'mutate (v.)' as 'to permanently change.' The book also notes a virus can mutate, or change its identity, to avoid detection.
explain.zh: 词汇表将「mutate（动词）」定义为「永久地改变」。书中也提到病毒可以变异，即改变身份以躲避察觉。

options.en: medicines made from microbes to fight infections
options.zh: 由微生物制造、用于对抗感染的药物

options.en: weak viruses used in vaccines according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 疫苗中使用的弱病毒（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines 'antibiotics' as 'medicines made from microbes to fight infections in the body.' The book explains some microbes naturally produce these strong medicines.
explain.zh: 词汇表将「抗生素」定义为「由微生物制造、用于对抗体内感染的药物」。书中解释说某些微生物会自然产生这些强效药物。

q.en: According to the book, about what percentage of diseases are transmitted through touch?
q.zh: 根据本书，大约有多少百分比的疾病是通过接触传播的？

options.en: 25 percent according to some versions of the story

explain.en: Under 'Keeping clean' the book says: 'Eighty percent of diseases are transmitted through touch because many people do not wash their hands.'
explain.zh: 在「保持清洁」一节，书中写道：「80%的疾病是通过接触传播的，因为许多人不洗手。」

q.en: What is the difference between an epidemic and a pandemic?
q.zh: 「流行病」（epidemic）和「大流行病」（pandemic）的区别是什么？

options.en: A pandemic is when an epidemic spreads around the world
options.zh: 大流行病是指流行病蔓延到全世界

options.en: They are exactly the same thing
An epidemic is worldwide while a pandemic is local according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 流行病是全球性的，而大流行病是局部性的（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book defines an epidemic as a disease rapidly spreading to a large number of people, and 'When an epidemic quickly spreads to infect larger groups of people around the world, it's called a pandemic.'
explain.zh: 书中将流行病定义为迅速传播给大量人群的疾病，「当流行病迅速蔓延到全世界更大的人群时，称为大流行病。」

q.en: Why does the author include the 'Do You Know?' note that Europeans brought smallpox to the Americas, killing millions of Native Americans?
q.zh: 作者为什么要加入「你知道吗？」段落，说欧洲人把天花带到美洲、导致数百万原住民死亡？

options.en: To explain how vaccines were invented
options.zh: 为了解释疫苗是如何发明的

options.en: To give a recipe for cooking food safely according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了提供安全烹饪食物的方法（按书中另一种说法）
为了描述巴斯德的实验

options.en: To show the deadly effect of a disease on people with no immunity
options.zh: 为了说明疾病对没有免疫力的人群造成的致命影响

explain.en: The book notes Native Americans 'had no immunity to it. As a result, smallpox killed millions,' showing how a disease with no natural resistance can devastate a population.
explain.zh: 书中提到原住民「对它没有任何免疫力。结果，天花导致数百万人死亡」，说明一种人们毫无抵抗力的疾病会如何摧毁一个族群。

q.en: What is the author's main message in the Conclusion of the book?
q.zh: 本书结论部分作者的主要观点是什么？

options.en: We can never eliminate all harmful microbes but can learn to control them and stay healthier
options.zh: 我们永远无法消灭所有有害微生物，但可以学会控制它们并保持健康

options.en: All microbes are harmful and must be destroyed according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 所有微生物都有害，必须被消灭（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Only antibiotics can cure every disease
options.zh: 只有抗生素能治愈所有疾病

explain.en: The Conclusion states: 'Even with all of this effort, we will never be able to wipe out all of the world's harmful microbes. But we can learn how they cause infection and can help to control them.'
explain.zh: 结论写道：「尽管付出了所有这些努力，我们永远无法消灭世界上所有有害的微生物。但我们可以了解它们如何引起感染，并帮助控制它们。」

prompt.en: Think about a time you were sick with a cold or the flu. What habits from the book's 'Staying Healthy' section do you already practice, and which could you improve?
prompt.zh: 回想你感冒或得流感的一次经历。书中「保持健康」一节的习惯里，你已经做到了哪些，又有哪些可以改进？

en: Mention hand-washing, covering coughs, and staying home when sick as habits from the book.
Reflect on why preventing the spread of germs matters for your family and community.
zh: 提及书中提到的洗手、咳嗽时遮挡、生病时居家等习惯。
思考预防病菌传播对家庭和社会的重要性。
